## WARNING (1)

### W-001: Chainsaw test `--since=120s` creates vacuous-pass risk
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/key-repository-mode/chainsaw-test.yaml:87` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: likely

**Problem**: The upstream `key_repository is world readable` warning fires once per uWSGI worker startup within seconds of pod creation. Step 3 only runs after Step 2's `Ready=True` succeeds, and on a slow CI cluster a Keystone CR may legitimately take 2–4 minutes (DB sync Job, bootstrap Job, then API Deployment rollout). If the keystone container started 3+ minutes before the `kubectl logs` call, `--since=120s` discards the startup window entirely, both `grep -cF` calls return 0, and the test passes vacuously even when a regression is present. The other 25 chainsaw tests in `tests/e2e/keystone/` use `--tail=N` only — this PR is the first to use `--since=`, with no rationale stated. `--tail=2000` already bounds the buffer; Keystone produces only a few hundred startup lines.

**Code**:
```
LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n openstack "$POD" -c keystone --tail=2000 --since=120s 2>&1)
```

**Action Required**: Drop the `--since=120s` window at line 87 and at the catch-block dump on line 123, keeping `--tail=2000` only. Update the catch-block heading on line 120 from `(full --since=120s)` to `(last 2000 lines)`.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
          LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n openstack "$POD" -c keystone --tail=2000 2>&1)
```

---

## INFO (7)

### I-001: Frontmatter `feature:` field introduces a new doc-meta convention
**File**: `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md:4` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: `grep -rln "^feature:" docs/` returns only this file. No other reference doc carries a `feature:` field, no schema or VitePress config consumes it, and there's no precedent for the convention. This PR also retroactively tags 20 prior CC tickets in addition to CC-0099 — pulling unrelated tickets into a single edit. The metadata is harmless but introduces a brand-new convention added to one file with no documentation. Cross-references the project pattern "Enforce ticket scope in PRs"; flagged as INFO since the metadata has no behavioral impact.

**Code**:
```
---
title: Keystone Reconciler Architecture
quadrant: operator
feature: CC-0013, CC-0015, CC-0038, CC-0057, CC-0058, CC-0064, CC-0065, CC-0067, CC-0068, CC-0071, CC-0072, CC-0073, CC-0074, CC-0077, CC-0078, CC-0079, CC-0080, CC-0081, CC-0087, CC-0095, CC-0099
---
```

**Action Required**: Remove the `feature:` line from the frontmatter (CC-0099 is already inline-tagged in the table rows that matter), or open a follow-up to standardise it across `docs/reference/`.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Remove the `feature:` line from the frontmatter, or open a follow-up issue to standardise the field across `docs/reference/` with a documented schema.
```

---

### I-002: Asymmetric integration-test coverage for credential rotation init container
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go:846` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The integration test asserts the new `install -m 0400` argv only for the fernet rotation CronJob. There is no integration-test coverage of `credentialRotationCronJob`'s init container — the existing `TestIntegration_CronJobDetailedSpec` suite is fernet-only. The new unit test `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_CopyKeysInitContainerPreservesNonWorldReadableMode` covers it at the unit level, so this is a coverage symmetry gap rather than a hole. The project patterns "Apply bug fixes to all instances of the same pattern" and "Apply symmetric validation to all similar inputs" apply if a credential integration test is added later.

**Code**:
```
g.Expect(initContainer.Command).To(Equal([]string{"sh", "-c", "install -m 0400 /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/"}))
```

**Action Required**: No fix required for this PR — flagged so the asymmetry is visible if a future integration test is added for credential rotation.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Optionally extend `TestIntegration_CronJobDetailedSpec` (or add a parallel test) to cover the credential rotation CronJob's init container argv with a matching assertion.
```

---

### I-003: PR description claims effective mode `0o450`; kubelet projection produces `0o440`
**File**: `PR description` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The PR body states *"effective file mode becomes `0450` after the FSGroup `| 050` projection"*. Kubelet's actual FSGroup projection mirrors the owner's permission bits onto the group via `mode | (mode & 0700) >> 3` — for `0400` input this yields `0440` (owner-read + group-read), not `0450` (which would imply group-execute). The code is correct (`DefaultMode=0o400`) and the resulting behaviour is what the test asserts; only the PR description mis-states the projected mode.

**Code**:
```
(PR body) effective file mode becomes `0450` after the FSGroup `| 050` projection
```

**Action Required**: Update the PR description to state the effective mode is `0440` (not `0450`) after FSGroup projection. No code change needed.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Edit the PR description to replace `0450` with `0440` and correct the projection formula to `mode | (mode & 0700) >> 3`.
```

---

### I-001: Positive Deviation: feature: front-matter added in full rather than appended
**File**: `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md:4` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: Spec said 'append `, CC-0099` to the front-matter `feature:` list at L4', but the field did not exist on origin/main (only `title:` and `quadrant:` were present). PR adds the entire field with 21 CC features ending in CC-0099: `feature: CC-0013, CC-0015, ..., CC-0099`. This is broader coverage than requested by the specification.

**Code**:
```
feature: CC-0013, CC-0015, ..., CC-0099
```

**Action Required**: No change needed — broader coverage than requested. Documents the doc's full feature provenance instead of just CC-0099.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
No change required. The deviation is positive — it documents the full feature provenance of the doc rather than only CC-0099, which is preferable.
```

### I-002: Positive Deviation: chainsaw log scrape uses --since=120s --tail=2000 (broader than spec)
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/key-repository-mode/chainsaw-test.yaml:87` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: Spec called for `kubectl logs <keystone-pod> -c keystone --tail=500` for 30 s. Implementation uses `kubectl logs ... --tail=2000 --since=120s` — wider line buffer and longer history window. The single-shot scrape after Ready=True reliably captures the early-startup warning that fires before the WSGI master fork.

**Code**:
```
kubectl logs <keystone-pod> -c keystone --tail=2000 --since=120s
```

**Action Required**: No change needed — strictly broader coverage; any warning within a 30s observation window is also captured by this 120s history window.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
No change required. The deviation is strictly broader: a 120s/2000-line window is a superset of the 30s/500-line window the spec requested.
```

---

### I-003: chainsaw failure message names CC-0099 + path + count, but does not echo the offending log line itself
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/key-repository-mode/chainsaw-test.yaml:92-94` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: REQ-006 scenario 'Test fails clearly when the warning regresses' calls for 'printing the offending log line'. The chainsaw assertion uses `grep -cF` (count only) and prints e.g. `ERROR (CC-0099 REQ-006): keystone container logged '...' ($FERNET_MATCHES occurrence(s))`. The actual matching log line is not echoed in the assertion error itself. The catch block at lines 113-138 does dump the full keystone container log via `kubectl logs --tail=2000 --since=120s`, so the offending line is still visible in failure diagnostics — but not in the immediate error message.

**Code**:
```
FERNET_MATCHES=$(... | grep -cF '...')
if [ "$FERNET_MATCHES" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "ERROR (CC-0099 REQ-006): keystone container logged 'key_repository is world readable: /etc/keystone/fernet-keys' ($FERNET_MATCHES occurrence(s))"
```

**Action Required**: Optional: change `grep -cF` to `grep -F` and capture matching lines into a variable, then print `Offending line(s): $MATCHES` before exiting non-zero. Not required — the catch block already provides full log diagnostics on failure.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Capture `grep -F` output (full matching lines) into a variable instead of just the count, and echo the variable contents before the non-zero exit so the offending log line appears directly in the assertion error rather than only in the catch-block log dump.
```

---

### I-004: Credential init-container argv shape (`sh`, `-c`, …) only loosely asserted in unit tests
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential_test.go:814` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: REQ-005 scenario 'Init-container shell command remains POSIX `sh -c`'. The credential unit test only checks `len(initContainer.Command) >= 3` and substring matches on `Command[2]`. It does not assert `Command[0] == "sh"` and `Command[1] == "-c"`. The fernet side has equivalent unit-test coverage plus a stricter integration assertion (`integration_test.go:846` uses full slice equality). Source code in `reconcile_credential.go:329` uses the canonical `[]string{"sh", "-c", ...}` shape, so the requirement is functionally met; only the credential unit test does not lock the argv shape.

**Code**:
```
g.Expect(len(initContainer.Command)).To(BeNumerically(">=", 3))
g.Expect(initContainer.Command[2]).To(ContainSubstring("install -m 0400"))
```

**Action Required**: Add explicit assertions on `Command[0]` and `Command[1]` so a future change to e.g. `bash` or a non-shell entrypoint fails the unit test the same way as the fernet integration test would.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
g.Expect(len(initContainer.Command)).To(BeNumerically(">=", 3))
g.Expect(initContainer.Command[0]).To(Equal("sh"))
g.Expect(initContainer.Command[1]).To(Equal("-c"))
g.Expect(initContainer.Command[2]).To(ContainSubstring("install -m 0400"))
```

---

## Summary

The PR cleanly implements a security-hardening change with strong symmetry across the three Pod templates mounting Fernet/credential keys, comprehensive unit-test coverage including regression guards on adjacent fields (PSS-restricted container context, ConfigMap volume modes), and a focused E2E test. The design choice of Pod-level `FSGroup` plus `DefaultMode: 0o400` is correct: kubelet rewrites mode `0400 → 0440` and chgrp's the volume to GID 42424, so the keystone process reads via the group bit while world bits are zero. The only material finding is the chainsaw test's `--since=120s` window, which makes the negative log assertion vacuously satisfiable on slow CI clusters. (includes adversarial review pass)

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 1 |
| INFO     | 7 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Merge after replacing `--tail=2000 --since=120s` with `--tail=2000` in the chainsaw test (and updating the catch-block heading). The remaining INFO findings (new `feature:` frontmatter convention, asymmetric integration coverage, PR-body mode mis-statement) are non-blocking and can be addressed in this PR or a follow-up at the author's discretion.
resolution_summary: Addressed the warning by removing `--since=120s` from the chainsaw log scrape (line 87) and the catch-block dump (line 131), and updating the catch-block heading to `(last 2000 lines)`. Applied the optional INFO-3 enhancement by switching from `grep -cF` (count only) to `grep -F` (capture lines) in `chainsaw-test.yaml` for both the fernet and credential paths, so the offending log lines are echoed directly in the assertion error. Applied the AUTO-FIX from INFO-4 by adding explicit `Command[0] == "sh"` and `Command[1] == "-c"` assertions in `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_CopyKeysInitContainerPreservesNonWorldReadableMode`. Addressed INFO-1 (frontmatter `feature:` field) per the reviewer's primary suggestion by removing the `feature:` line from `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` — no other reference doc carries the field, no schema consumes it, and CC-0099 is already inline-tagged where it matters. The remaining ASK-fix INFO findings (asymmetric integration test, PR-body `0450` mis-statement) were intentionally left as-is per the reviewer's non-blocking guidance; the integration-test asymmetry was flagged for visibility only.
